findings.title: Improve Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
findings.description: Address the 15% drop in search relevance scores for anonymous users by adjusting the ranking algorithm to better accommodate non-authenticated queries.
assumptions: The current ranking algorithm is not suitable for anonymous users.

evidence.excerpt: Our p50 search relevance score fell from 0.85 to 0.72 immediately following the v2.4.1 release.

recommendations: Re-evaluate the ranking algorithm for anonymous users.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an anonymous user WHEN they perform a search THEN the search relevance score should improve to at least 0.80.

findings.title: Optimize Mobile Search Latency
findings.description: Reduce mobile search latency from 890ms to below 500ms to enhance user experience.
assumptions: The increased latency is due to the new ranking model.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms.

recommendations: Profile the new ranking model and implement caching for mobile sessions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a mobile user WHEN they perform a search THEN the search latency should be less than 500ms.

findings.title: Fix Stale Autocomplete Suggestions
findings.description: Correct the configuration issue causing stale autocomplete suggestions to improve user satisfaction.
assumptions: The cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions was incorrectly set.

evidence.excerpt: The cache TTL was inadvertently extended to 7 days.

recommendations: Reset the cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions to 24 hours.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user types in the search bar WHEN autocomplete suggestions are displayed THEN all suggestions should be current and relevant.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: Implement instrumentation for image search to establish baseline metrics for performance evaluation.
assumptions: Instrumentation for image search was not included in the v2.4.1 release.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Add analytics events for image search queries.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an image search is performed WHEN the search is executed THEN the relevant analytics events should be logged.

findings.title: Re-run A/B Tests for Anonymous Users
findings.description: Conduct targeted A/B tests for the new ranking algorithm focusing on anonymous users to gather relevant data.
assumptions: Previous A/B tests did not adequately represent anonymous users.

evidence.excerpt: The test was not powered for the anonymous-user segment separately.

recommendations: Design and execute A/B tests specifically for anonymous users.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an anonymous user is part of the A/B test WHEN they perform a search THEN their search relevance score should be tracked and analyzed.

findings.title: Implement Monitoring for Search Performance
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for search performance metrics to proactively identify issues.
assumptions: Current monitoring does not cover all search performance metrics.

evidence.excerpt: No SLO breach yet but trending toward it.

recommendations: Implement monitoring for search latency and relevance scores.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN monitoring is implemented WHEN search performance metrics are collected THEN alerts should trigger for any anomalies.

findings.title: Track Session Abandonment After Search
findings.description: Implement tracking for session abandonment rates post-search to gauge user satisfaction.
assumptions: Understanding abandonment rates will help identify search issues.

evidence.excerpt: A high abandonment rate may indicate dissatisfaction with search results.

recommendations: Add tracking for session abandonment metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a search is performed WHEN the session is tracked THEN abandonment rates should be recorded and reported.

findings.title: Improve Search Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: Enhance the search results to improve the click-through rate based on user engagement metrics.
assumptions: Improving relevance will lead to higher CTR.

evidence.excerpt: The Search Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of search results that users click on.

recommendations: Analyze and optimize search result presentation.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a search result is displayed WHEN a user clicks on it THEN the CTR should increase by at least 5%.

findings.title: Conduct User Feedback Collection
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on search results to inform future improvements.
assumptions: User feedback will provide insights into search relevance issues.

evidence.excerpt: Users are reporting that top results no longer match their intent.

recommendations: Design a user feedback collection tool for search results.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user interacts with search results WHEN they provide feedback THEN their input should be recorded and analyzed.

findings.title: Implement Security Measures for Search Queries
findings.description: Ensure that all search queries are secured to protect user data and comply with privacy regulations.
assumptions: User data must be protected during search operations.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in multiple sources.

recommendations: Review and enhance security protocols for search queries.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a search query is submitted WHEN it is processed THEN user data should be encrypted and secured.

findings.title: Enhance Accessibility for Search Features
findings.description: Ensure that all search features are accessible to users with disabilities, complying with WCAG standards.
assumptions: Accessibility improvements will enhance user experience for all.

evidence.excerpt: Detected accessibility risk keywords in one source.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit of search features.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with disabilities accesses the search feature WHEN they use assistive technologies THEN all functionalities should be operable and compliant with WCAG 2.1 standards.

summary: The analysis identified critical issues affecting search relevance and performance following the v2.4.1 deployment. Key requirements have been defined to address these issues, including improvements to search relevance for anonymous users, optimization of mobile search latency, and fixing stale autocomplete suggestions. Additionally, metrics tracking, user feedback mechanisms, and security measures have been outlined to ensure a comprehensive approach to enhancing the SearchBoost Query Engine.
